focus: correctness: renderer behavior, fluid CSS assumptions, fallback behavior, and regression proof
findingsSummary: One P1: unsupported cqi invalidates variable-backed consumers instead of preserving fixed shipped sizes. One P2: substring regression test does not lock named-container wiring or both refinements.

findingDetails.title: No-container-query fallback does not preserve shipped fixed type sizes
findingDetails.scopeProof: All four cqi-bearing custom properties and their consumers occur in the inspected _CSS block; no fixed fallback or @supports guard was found.
findingDetails.claim: Unsupported cqi makes variable-backed consumers invalid rather than selecting clamp bounds.
findingDetails.counterevidenceSought: Checked fixed fallbacks, @supports guards, and the fixed body font; body containment does not preserve prior per-surface sizes or font shorthand properties.
findingDetails.alternativeExplanation: Modern Chromium webviews support cqi, confining impact to the explicitly promised unsupported path.
findingDetails.downgradeTrigger: Requirement removal or engine evidence that these unsupported cqi values resolve to clamp bounds.

findingDetails.title: Regression test does not lock named-container wiring or both refinements
findingDetails.scopeProof: The only focused test has three substring assertions and omits container-name:report plus both breakpoint signatures.

traceabilityChecks.result: Unsupported-engine fallback contradicts CSS computed-value behavior.

noveltyJustification: All six severity-weighted points are new in this first iteration; no prior findings existed.
edgeCases: Modern Chromium likely follows the supported path.
Legacy-browser execution unavailable; CSS semantics and direct declarations support the finding.
Structural-impact analysis not needed because exact producer and test symbols were supplied.
ruledOut: No active supported-path renderer failure in inspected declarations.
Test gap remains P2 because current wiring is correct.

reason: Exact producer and test symbols were supplied; direct reads provided evidence.
Named container and both refinement blocks are present in the inspected implementation.
Impact is presentation fallback loss in an explicitly secondary compatibility path.
